{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether ACE2 is expressed on cell surfaces. Multiple documents from the MEDCORP source explicitly state that the SARS-CoV-2 virus binds to the angiotensin-converting enzyme 2 (ACE2) receptor on the surface of host cells. For example, Document [1] states that SARS-CoV-2 invades host cells by binding its spike protein to ACE2 receptors expressed on the surface epithelium of various organs. Document [2] confirms that ACE2 receptors are expressed by epithelial cells in the respiratory tract, gastrointestinal tract, myocardial cells, kidney, and bladder. Document [17] notes that ACE2 receptors are expressed highly in the nasal, pharyngeal, and bronchial mucosa and are the primary source of entry for the virus. Document [25] also states that ACE2 expression is predominant in respiratory mucosal and alveolar epithelial cells but is also found in other tissues. These consistent references across multiple sources confirm that ACE2 is expressed on cell surfaces. The UMLS source does not provide direct information on ACE2 expression on cell surfaces, and its content focuses on drug mechanisms and chemical relationships, not cellular receptor expression. Therefore, based on the MEDCORP documents, ACE2 is clearly expressed on cell surfaces.", "answer_choice": "A"}
